verses.text: 'I am sought of them that asked not for me,' God says, having stretched out His hands all day 'unto a rebellious people.' Grace seeks the unseeking. Paul applies this to the calling of the Gentiles and Israel's refusal.

'I am sought of them that asked not for me,' God says, having stretched out His hands all day 'unto a rebellious people.' Grace seeks the unseeking. Paul applies this to the calling of the Gentiles and Israel's refusal.
They provoke Him with idolatry and self-righteousness, saying, 'I am holier than thou.' Their religion is proud and false. Nothing offends God more than sin cloaked in self-righteous pride.
They provoke Him with idolatry and self-righteousness, saying, 'I am holier than thou.' Their religion is proud and false. Nothing offends God more than sin cloaked in self-righteous pride.
They provoke Him with idolatry and self-righteousness, saying, 'I am holier than thou.' Their religion is proud and false. Nothing offends God more than sin cloaked in self-righteous pride.
'It is written before me,' God says; He will repay their iniquities and their fathers' together. Sin is recorded. The patience of God does not erase the account He keeps.
'It is written before me,' God says; He will repay their iniquities and their fathers' together. Sin is recorded. The patience of God does not erase the account He keeps.
As good grapes are kept in a poor cluster, so God will not destroy all but 'bring forth a seed out of Jacob.' A remnant is spared. For the sake of the faithful few, God preserves a people.
As good grapes are kept in a poor cluster, so God will not destroy all but 'bring forth a seed out of Jacob.' A remnant is spared. For the sake of the faithful few, God preserves a people.
As good grapes are kept in a poor cluster, so God will not destroy all but 'bring forth a seed out of Jacob.' A remnant is spared. For the sake of the faithful few, God preserves a people.
But those who forsake Him and set tables for false gods He will 'number... to the sword,' for they chose evil. Judgment answers their choice. Those who abandon God reap the ruin they elected.
But those who forsake Him and set tables for false gods He will 'number... to the sword,' for they chose evil. Judgment answers their choice. Those who abandon God reap the ruin they elected.

His servants shall eat, drink, and rejoice while the rebellious hunger and cry; the faithful shall have a new name and bless 'the God of truth.' Two destinies divide them. God distinguishes between those who serve Him and those who forsake Him.

His servants shall eat, drink, and rejoice while the rebellious hunger and cry; the faithful shall have a new name and bless 'the God of truth.' Two destinies divide them. God distinguishes between those who serve Him and those who forsake Him.

His servants shall eat, drink, and rejoice while the rebellious hunger and cry; the faithful shall have a new name and bless 'the God of truth.' Two destinies divide them. God distinguishes between those who serve Him and those who forsake Him.

His servants shall eat, drink, and rejoice while the rebellious hunger and cry; the faithful shall have a new name and bless 'the God of truth.' Two destinies divide them. God distinguishes between those who serve Him and those who forsake Him.

'Behold, I create new heavens and a new earth,' the former forgotten; God will rejoice in Jerusalem. A wholly new order dawns. The promise reaches to the renewed creation where God delights in His people.

'Behold, I create new heavens and a new earth,' the former forgotten; God will rejoice in Jerusalem. A wholly new order dawns. The promise reaches to the renewed creation where God delights in His people.

'Behold, I create new heavens and a new earth,' the former forgotten; God will rejoice in Jerusalem. A wholly new order dawns. The promise reaches to the renewed creation where God delights in His people.

There shall be no untimely death, but long life, secure homes, and labour that is not in vain; 'mine elect shall long enjoy the work of their hands.' Curse gives way to blessing. The new world undoes the frustrations of the old.

There shall be no untimely death, but long life, secure homes, and labour that is not in vain; 'mine elect shall long enjoy the work of their hands.' Curse gives way to blessing. The new world undoes the frustrations of the old.

'Before they call, I will answer'; the wolf and lamb feed together, and none shall hurt in all God's holy mountain. Prayer is answered and peace restored. The new creation is a place of harmony and ready grace.

'Before they call, I will answer'; the wolf and lamb feed together, and none shall hurt in all God's holy mountain. Prayer is answered and peace restored. The new creation is a place of harmony and ready grace.
